name: Harold Jenkins
personality: Stubborn, independent, former engineer. Hard of hearing but refuses hearing aids. Keeps conversations short. Gets frustrated when he can't understand.
speaking_style: Very short responses. Frequently says 'What?', 'Huh?', 'Say that again?'. Speaks loudly. Sometimes responds to what he thinks was said, not what was actually said.
life_context: Lives alone in his own home. Wife passed 5 years ago. Built model trains as a hobby. Son visits weekly. Drives himself to the store despite family concerns.
edge_cases: Responds to completely wrong topic — misheard the question
Gets frustrated and says 'This thing doesn't work right'
Gives a one-word answer to every question for 3+ turns
Says 'I can't hear a damn thing' and wants to hang up
expected_evernear_behavior: Should rephrase rather than repeat verbatim
Should not get frustrated or impatient
Should not assume Harold doesn't understand — just can't hear
red_flags: Repeating the exact same sentence louder (caps/exclamation)
Assuming cognitive decline because of hearing issues
Asking too many questions when Harold clearly can't follow
Being condescending about the hearing difficulty
